data.title: Bonterra Resources launches $7M private placement offering with flow-through options
data.description:
• Bonterra Resources (TSXV:BTR:CA) is conducting a brokered private placement for up to ~$7M in gross proceeds.
  • The offering includes up to 20.45M Hard-Dollar Units at $0.22, 2.08M Flow-Through Units at $0.24, and 6.56M Premium Flow-Through Units at $0.305.
  • Each unit includes one common share and one-half warrant exercisable at $0.30 for three years.
  • Closing is expected on or around June 30, 2025.
  • FT and Premium FT proceeds will fund qualifying Canadian exploration expenses; HD Unit proceeds will support general corporate use.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Boeing's stock falls as Air India crash raises more safety questions
data.description:
Boeing’s (NYSE:BA) stock fell as much as 5.9% on Thursday, while suppliers GE Aerospace (NYSE:GE) and Spirit AeroSystems Holdings (NYSE:SPR) slipped as much as 2.8% and 3.4%, respectively, after one of Boeing’s 787 Dreamliners crashed in India on Thursday.

The incident raises fresh questions about the safety of Boeing’s (NYSE:BA) planes.

The Air India flight, a 787-8 model on its way to London’s Gatwick Airport, went down shortly after departing from Ahmedabad, a major city in western India. The aircraft, which had 242 people on board, crashed into a residential neighborhood not far from the airport, triggering a massive emergency response, according to multiple press reports.

Only one passenger survived, a British national of Indian origin who was being treated for injuries. 

Air India said the flight took off at 1:38 p.m. local time and was carrying 230 passengers. Among them were 169 Indian nationals, 53 from the U.K., one Canadian and seven from Portugal.

“The injured are being taken to the nearest hospitals,” the airline said in a statement.

The Dreamliner, introduced in 2011, had maintained a strong safety record until this incident, with no previous fatal accidents.

Boeing (NYSE:BA) acknowledged the reports, stating,  “We are in contact with Air India regarding Flight 171 and stand ready to support them. Our thoughts are with the passengers, crew, first responders and all affected.”

GE Aerospace (NYSE:GE) is one of the manufacturers of jet engines for the 787, while Spirit AeroSystems (NYSE:SPR) produces main sections of the plane.

Footage from Indian news outlets showed thick black smoke billowing above the skyline and emergency workers transporting victims on stretchers. Flight data from Flightradar24 indicated the jet had reached just 625 feet in altitude and was descending at 475 feet per minute when its location signal was lost. The aircraft was 11 years old.

The crash site lies in Gujarat, a state known for its economic growth and home to many of India’s top business families. It is also the home state of Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

“It is heartbreaking beyond words. In this sad hour, my thoughts are with everyone affected by it,” Modi posted on X, formerly Twitter. He added that federal assistance was being provided to local officials in Ahmedabad.

Local police confirmed the plane crashed in a populated area after departing from the city’s main airport, according to several press reports.

Air India, once state-owned, has been operated by Tata Sons since 2022, after the Indian government divested the struggling airline. Natarajan Chandrasekaran, chairman of Air India, confirmed the accident in a message on X, writing that Flight 171 was “involved in a tragic accident today.”

High-profile safety incidents

This disaster adds to an already difficult year for the aviation sector, which has seen multiple high-profile incidents. In January, a passenger jet and a military helicopter collided near Washington, D.C.

Boeing (NYSE:BA) is still working to recover from the reputational damage caused by a widely publicized incident early last year, when a door panel detached mid-flight from a newly delivered 737 Max.

That event led to the departure of the company’s then-CEO and triggered renewed concerns about Boeing’s (NYSE:BA) approach to safety and ongoing manufacturing flaws across its production facilities.

The aircraft involved in Thursday’s crash was not part of a recent delivery, it originally entered service in 2014, according to flight-tracking data from Flightradar24.

The Dreamliner, named through a public voting campaign that drew nearly 500,000 responses, is one of Boeing’s (NYSE:BA) most popular widebody jets, with nearly 1,200 units delivered to date.

Despite its commercial success, the 787 has experienced its share of manufacturing challenges. It was hampered by supply chain delays and battery malfunctions in its early years, issues that led regulators to ground the entire fleet temporarily in 2013. More recently, Boeing (NYSE:BA) suspended deliveries for almost two years due to quality-control concerns.

Production has since been ramped up, with Boeing (BA) executives reporting progress in streamlining manufacturing and enhancing workforce training. These efforts followed a slowdown intended to fix systemic issues on the factory floor.

Kelly Ortberg, who became chief executive in August, has warned employees that the company remains in a vulnerable position and cannot afford further missteps.

Earlier this month, Boeing (BA) agreed to pay $1.1 billion as part of a settlement to avoid criminal charges related to two earlier 737 Max crashes that occurred in 2018 and 2019, killing over 300 people. Under the terms of the deal, Boeing (BA) will allocate $455 million toward improving its safety, compliance and quality systems, and an additional $444.5 million will go to victims' families under a separate settlement agreement.

U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer expressed sympathy for those affected, saying, “The scenes emerging of a London-bound plane carrying many British nationals crashing in the Indian city of Ahmedabad are devastating.”

data.title: Perma-Pipe International appoints Saleh Sagr as president, CEO
data.description:
• Perma-Pipe International (NASDAQ:PPIH) has appointed Saleh Sagr as its president and CEO, following the departure of David Mansfield, effective June 7, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Hapbee upgrades US listing to OTCID to expand investor access, increase market visibility
data.description:
• Hapbee Technologies (TSXV:HAPB:CA) said on Thursday that its U.S. listing has been upgraded from the OTC Pink Sheets to the newly established OTCID (OTC International Designated) tier on the OTC Markets Group platform.
  • The company will continue to trade under the symbol HAPBF once the upgrade is complete, effective July 1, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Merck Animal Health gets EU backing for Numelvi tablets
data.description:
• Merck Animal Health, a unit of Merck & Co (NYSE:MRK) said that the European Medicines Agency’s Committee for Veterinary Medicinal Products (CVMP) recommended the approval of Numelvi tablets for dogs.
  • Based on the CVMP’s recommendation, the European Commission is expected to issue a decision for marketing authorization during the third quarter of 2025.

data.title: Flowco authorizes $50M stock repurchase program
data.description: Flowco Holdings (NYSE:FLOC) announced a $50M stock repurchase program. The buyback targets the Company’s Class A common stock. Source: Press release

data.title: RH beats Q1 expectations, keeps guidance unchanged despite tariff uncertainties
data.description:
Shares of RH (NYSE:RH) are rallying in Thursday’s after-hours trading as the company’s efforts to increase investments in Europe, expand and elevate its product offerings, and mitigate tariff headwinds delivered better-than-expected results for the first quarter.

For the quarter ended May 3, the luxury home furnishings' retailer earned an adjusted profit of $0.13 per share. This compares to a loss of $0.40 in the same quarter last year and beat expectations for a loss of $0.07. And despite operating in the “worst housing market in 50 years,” sales were up 12% to an above-consensus $814M.

Adjusted operating margin improved by 50 basis points to 7% while adjusted EBITDA margin increased 80 basis points to 13.1%, both of which were at the high end of the company’s expectations.

Looking ahead to the current quarter and fiscal year 2025, RH (NYSE:RH) continues to take steps to mitigate the impact of U.S. import tariff with a goal to shift sourcing from China to just 2% from 16%, currently, with a “meaningful portion” of costs to be absorbed by vendors.

The company will also delay the launch of the new concept planned for the second half of 2025 to the spring of 2026 when there is more certainty regarding tariffs.

Additionally, due to the “significant and unexpected” Liberation Day tariffs announced on April 2, the disruption in shipments and resourcing efforts will negatively impact revenues by ~6 points in Q2 and will be recovered in the second half of the year.  

For the second quarter, revenue is expected to increase by 8% to 10%, translating into Q2 revenue of $896.03M to $912.63M, below the $915.8M estimate. Adjusted operating margin is pegged for 15% to 16% and adjusted operating margin 15% to 16%.

The company expects FY25 revenue growth of 10% to 13%, translating to $3.5B to $3.6B versus $3.52B estimates. Adjusted operating margin is expected to be between 14% to 15% and adjusted EBITDA margin of 20% to 21%. Free cash flow is targeted to be between $250M to $350M.

“While there remains uncertainty until the reciprocal tariff negotiations are complete, we have proven we are well positioned to compete favorably in any market conditions,” the company said in a statement.
